description: Analyze space consumption inequity in San Francisco by creating a Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/space_consumption.ipynb. Using data from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5 (households, buildings, parcels, and zones), compute the residential square footage per capita across 10 household income deciles and aggregate by geographic zones. The analysis requires filtering for households with valid person counts and unit square footage (between 100 and 20,000 sqft, derived from sqft_per_unit or building_sqft/residential_units). Calculate sqft_per_person for each household and segment them into 10 income deciles. Export a summary of median income, median unit sqft, median sqft per person, and household count for each decile to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/space_by_income_decile.csv. Export median sqft per person and household count grouped by zone_id (excluding zones with fewer than 50 households) to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/space_by_zone.csv. Generate a bar chart of median sqft per person by income decile saved to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/space_inequity_chart.png. Calculate the Top-to-Bottom Space Ratio (Decile 10 median sqft per person divided by Decile 1 median sqft per person) and save the result to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/space_ratio_report.txt.
